title: The prompt that got me a job offer at OpenAI
post:
I wrote a prompt. It got me a job offer at OpenAI.

No exaggeration.

During my interview, I was given a real problem: how would you design an AI assistant that helps doctors communicate with patients? I didn't just describe an approach. I built a working prototype using prompt chaining, evaluation frameworks, and safety constraints.

The hiring manager said my prompt design showed deeper understanding of AI limitations than most candidates with years of experience.

What I learned: prompt engineering isn't about being clever. It's about being precise about what you want and explicit about what can go wrong.

The job market for prompt engineers is exploding. Companies are realizing that the difference between a useful AI and a useless one is often one well-written prompt.

Interested in breaking into this field?
